repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
<!--
  Thank you for contributing to Neovim!
  If this is your first time, check out https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests-prs
  for our PR guidelines.
-->

## Problem
`parser_gc()` calls `ts_parser_delete()` but leaves the userdata pointer pointing to freed memory. If the GC finalizer runs at an unexpected time (e.g. inside `nvim_buf_get_lines` as seen in #39411), a stale pointer could cause a crash.

## Solution
- NULL out `*ud` after `ts_parser_delete()` in `parser_gc()`
- Update `parser_check()` to handle NULL with a clear error message, guarding all parser methods against UAF

Fixes #39411

~~Draft - opening to run CI and discuss approach before requesting review.~~

comment_data.body:
why is this type changed (just curious)

also in my experience if it's ready mark it ready so there's least possible friction to get your prs up and in

I've changed type to `int` to align with the `luaL_checkudata` signature which takes an `int`. Small cleanup while I was touching the function.

Also, thanks for the advice, but I'm keeping is as draft on purpose; until @justinmk has a chance to look - we discussed the approach in #39411 and I want his sign-off before requesting the final review
I usually ignore Draft PRs (that's why Draft is useful for reviewers :)

don't think this needs to be changed? or change it to `Parser not found` ?

 "has been deleted" assumes that it existed, is that a valid assumption?

does it work if you change this to:


```suggestion
        parser:parse(nil, 'int x;', false)
```

and then use `t.pcall_err(exec_lua, function() ...)` in the test call, so we can assert the message more clearly
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -107,6 +107,27 @@ describe('treesitter parser API', function()
     )
   end)
 
+  it('ignores repeated parser finalization', function()
+    eq(
+      { false, true },
+      exec_lua(function()
+        vim.treesitter.language.add('c')
+
+        local parser = vim._create_ts_parser('c')
+        -- The parser metatable exposes __gc through __index, so this simulates a finalizer
+        -- running before all Lua references to the userdata are gone.
+        parser:__gc()
+        parser:__gc()
+
+        local ok, err = pcall(function()
+          parser:parse(nil, 'int x;', false)
+        end)
+        return { ok, tostring(err):find('Parser has been deleted', 1, true) ~= nil }

comment_data.body: original message implied that the wrong type was passed, but after the GC fix this check also triggers when the parser was valid but ha since been finalized - the userdata is a TSParser, its just been deleted. I think "Parser has been deleted" tells the caller exactly what happened and why the operation failed. "Parser not found" sounds to me like a lookup failure rather than lifecycle issue. Happy to change the wording if you have a preference, though
if the message will make sense then no problem
That's cleaner. I'll update the test. Will push the update shortly.

comment_data.body: I think we generally prefer the `uint*_t` types where we can use them.

comment_regular_data.body: CI is fine (without that one flake). What do you think of this approach @justinmk?

This test catches the bug: it passes on this PR and crashes on the parent commit.

```patch
diff --git a/test/functional/treesitter/parser_spec.lua b/test/functional/treesitter/parser_spec.lua
index 8678761e6e..9704507411 100644
--- a/test/functional/treesitter/parser_spec.lua
+++ b/test/functional/treesitter/parser_spec.lua
@@ -107,6 +107,27 @@ describe('treesitter parser API', function()
     )
   end)
 
+  it('ignores repeated parser finalization', function()
+    eq(
+      { false, true },
+      exec_lua(function()
+        vim.treesitter.language.add('c')
+
+        local parser = vim._create_ts_parser('c')
+        -- The parser metatable exposes __gc through __index, so this simulates a finalizer
+        -- running before all Lua references to the userdata are gone.
+        parser:__gc()
+        parser:__gc()
+
+        local ok, err = pcall(function()
+          parser:parse(nil, 'int x;', false)
+        end)
+        return { ok, tostring(err):find('Parser has been deleted', 1, true) ~= nil }
+      end)
+    )
+    assert_alive()
+  end)
+
   it('respects eol settings when parsing buffer', function()
     insert([[
       int main() {
```
Thanks @lewis6991 for providing the test case! I've verified it locally and added it to the PR.

title: fix(treesitter): zero parser userdata pointer after ts_parser_delete
